items.q: Why is 'Yo gusto el fútbol' wrong for 'I like football'?
items.correct: Gustar works backwards — the thing liked is the subject: Me gusta el fútbol.
items.misconception: It should be 'Yo gusto al fútbol' because gustar needs 'a'.
items.random_wrong: Because fútbol is a feminine noun.
items.why: Students map gustar word-for-word onto English 'like' instead of treating it as 'football is pleasing to me'.

items.q: What does describing a film as 'sensible' mean in Spanish?
items.why: 'Sensible' is a false friend meaning sensitive; the English 'sensible' is 'sensato' in Spanish.

items.q: What does 'asistir a un concierto' mean?
items.misconception: To assist (help out) at a concert.
items.why: False friend — asistir means to attend; 'to assist' in the helping sense is ayudar.

items.q: 'Me gustan las películas de terror' means:
items.misconception: I like the terror of films.
items.why: It's I like horror films.. “I like the terror of films.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Me gustan los conciertos.. “Me gusta los conciertos.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: A song. “A dance” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: At which Spanish celebration do people traditionally eat twelve grapes at midnight?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: La Nochevieja (New Year's Eve). “La Tomatina” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Un grupo tiene cuatro cantantes y un batería. How many members does the band have?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 members. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
